channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: If you let your flood insurance lapse and then got hit by Helene, you may be able to renew it
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Residents in the states hit by Hurricane Helene who had coverage through the federal flood insurance program but let it lapse before the storm hit may be able to renew it and still be covered from the impact.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: U.S. expands sanctions on Iran in response to its ballistic attack on Israel
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. on Friday announced new sanctions on Iran's energy sector in response to its Oct. 1 attack on Israel when it fired roughly 180 missiles into the country.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: California Senate passes controversial fuel reserves bill
item.description: California lawmakers in the State Senate approved a controversial bill requiring oil refineries to keep a minimum supply of fuel reserves to avoid future supply shortages and price hikes for consumers. The bill, spearheaded by Gov.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 3 in 5 millennials want full access to abortion: Study
item.description: The largest percentile of individuals who do not want to live in a "fully legal" abortion state is Gen Z at 33%, followed by Gen X and Baby Boomers (30%).
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Kern County District 2 Supervisors debate recap
item.description: BAKERSFIELD, Calif. (KGET) -- There's a crowded debate stage, a reflection of the packed candidate field, in the race to replace former Supervisor Zack Scrivner in Kern County's 2nd District.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Huh, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Vladimir Putin meets with Iranian President Pezeshkian to celebrate 'very close' relationship
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin met with Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian on Friday, hailing the "very close" relationship between Russia and Iran.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Third-party candidate Cornel West loses bid to get on Pennsylvania's presidential ballot
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — A federal judge has turned down Cornel West's request to be included on the presidential ballot in the key battleground state of Pennsylvania, expressing sympathy for his claim but saying it's too close to Election Day to make changes. U.S. District Judge J.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Abortion has passed inflation as the top election issue for women under 30, survey finds
item.description: A survey has found that abortion has passed inflation to become the top issue in the presidential election for women younger than 30.
item.dc:creator: Geoff Mulvihill, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Senate candidates steer clear of debates
item.description: Senate candidates this cycle are largely steering clear of the debate stage, in some cases avoiding it altogether, marking a major change from past years as campaigns question the debates' importance. For years, it was common for top Senate races to feature multiple debates.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Why do news media call the winners of elections?
item.description: It’s election night, the polls have closed and chances are you’re waiting on The Associated Press or one of the major television networks to say who will be the next president.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Milton's gone, but the political storm keeps raging over federal government's hurricane efforts
item.description: There's no let up in the war of words between President Biden and former President Trump over the government's response to dangerous hurricanes that slammed into the southeast.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: In Pacific Northwest, 2 toss-up US House races could determine control of narrowly divided Congress
item.description: PORTLAND, Ore. (AP) — In their battle for Congress, national Republicans and Democrats are keenly eyeing the Pacific Northwest, where two of the most competitive U.S. House races in the country are playing out.
item.dc:creator: CLAIRE RUSH and HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Back-to-back hurricanes reshape 2024 campaign’s final stretch
item.description: A pair of unwelcome and destructive guests named Helene and Milton have stormed their way into this year’s presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Utah candidates for Mitt Romney's open US Senate seat debate approaches to climate change
item.description: OGDEN, Utah (AP) — The Republican and Democratic candidates vying for Mitt Romney’s open U.S. Senate seat sought to convince Utah voters on Thursday that they have the better blueprint to tackle climate change and protect the state's vast natural resources. Republican U.S. Rep.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 5 takeaways from the Maryland Senate debate
item.description: Former Maryland Gov. Larry Hogan (R) and Prince George’s County Executive Angela Alsobrooks (D) sparred in their lone debate this cycle Thursday night as they look to replace retiring Sen. Ben Cardin (D-Md.) next year.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's complaints about '60 Minutes' put a spotlight on editing at the nation's top newsmagazine
item.description: Donald Trump skipped a “60 Minutes” interview this past week, but he's attacking CBS for how it handled its session with opponent Kamala Harris, calling it a “giant fake news scam” and suggesting the network was out to protect her.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Shondra Summers-Armstrong discusses key issues in Las Vegas city council race
item.description: LAS VEGAS (KLAS) — Shondra Summers-Armstrong, currently serving as an Assemblywoman for District 6, is running for Las Vegas City Council, Ward 5.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump insults Detroit while campaigning in the city
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — Former President Donald Trump criticized Detroit while delivering remarks to an economic group there on Thursday, saying the whole country would end up like the city if his Democratic opponent, Vice President Kamala Harris, is elected.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI and ISABELLA VOLMERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Interim NYPD Commissioner Tom Donlon could resign: sources
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Less than a month after the NYPD's interim commissioner was appointed, another major shake-up could be coming. Interim NYPD Commissioner Tom Donlon is expected to step down, sources said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, NewsNation

item.title: Trump visits Aurora after Venezuelan gang claims
item.description: The Trump campaign called Aurora a "war zone" in its rally announcement. The city's Republican mayor disagrees.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: Matisyahu says he is voting for Trump
item.description: "I just feel that the Jews are like, under attack, and that the current administration really doesn't care," Matisyahu said.
item.dc:creator: Paula Froelich, NewsNation

item.title: Harris accepts invite to CNN town hall as another Trump debate appears unlikely
item.description: Vice President Harris has accepted an invitation from CNN to participate in a town hall on the network a week before Election Day, as it appears unlikely that former President Trump will agree to a second debate with her.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, NewsNation

item.title: 5 takeaways from the Arizona Senate debate
item.description: Rep. Ruben Gallego (D-Ariz.) and Republican candidate Kari Lake faced off in their only debate in the Arizona Senate race Wednesday, and the two contenders frequently traded barbs. Gallego and Lake, who are vying for retiring Sen.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, NewsNation

item.title: Ryan Hamilton discusses homelessness, the Animal Foundation, and F1
item.description: Ryan Hamilton, a Republican candidate for the Clark County Commission, appeared on 8 News Now's Daily Voting Guide to share his perspective on homelessness, animal welfare, and Formula One's impact on the local economy as the election nears.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pat Ryan, Alison Esposito face off in NY-18 debate on PIX11
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Democratic U.S. Rep. Pat Ryan and Republican Alison Esposito, the two candidates running to represent New York's 18th Congressional District, faced off in a debate on PIX11 Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11

item.title: Mayor Frey vetoes carbon emission fee adopted by city council
item.description: In his veto letter Minneapolis Mayor Jacob Frey said he agrees with the aims of the fee on polluters, but he said the council’s action amounts to an “unauthorized tax.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Thamer, MPR News

item.title: Trump campaigns in 'make or break' Pennsylvania
item.description: Former President Trump barnstormed Pennsylvania on Wednesday as he and his campaign have made clear the commonwealth could make or break his chances of winning in November. Trump held rallies in President Biden’s hometown of Scranton and in Reading.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Human Rights Campaign launching ad blitz for Harris, other Democrats
item.description: Human Rights Campaign (HRC) and its Equality Votes political action committee are launching a multimillion-dollar digital ad blitz aimed at reaching "equality voters" for the Harris-Walz ticket in key battleground states, the group announced Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: The presidential campaigns have made over 200 visits but speak to just a fraction of American voters
item.description: The nation's unique Electoral College system for electing a president, which replaces the popular vote, puts disproportionate voting power in the hands of a relative few states.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Brown says no to pro-Palestinian students' demands for divestment
item.description: Supporters of divestment ended their encampment last spring in exchange for a promise that their proposal for divestment would get a vote from the board this fall.
item.dc:creator: Tovia Smith, MPR News

item.title: How to watch the NY-18 Debate: Ryan vs. Esposito
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Candidates in the race for New York’s 18th Congressional District will face off in a debate Wednesday night. Democratic incumbent Congressman Pat Ryan will take on Republican challenger Alison Esposito in a 60-minute debate moderated by PIX11’s Henry Rosoff.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Jack, PIX 11

item.title: Ethel Kennedy, widow of Robert F. Kennedy, hospitalized after suffering stroke
item.description: "She is comfortable, she is getting the best care possible, and she is surrounded by family," Former Rep. Joe Kennedy III posted on social media.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Florida Republican suggests Greene have ‘head examined’ over hurricane claims
item.description: Florida Republican Rep. Carlos A. Gimenez suggested Wednesday that Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene (R-Ga.) should have her “head examined” after she claimed humans can control the weather.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Michigan US Senate debate: Rogers, Slotkin race could determine control
item.description: Rogers and Slotkin meet on Tuesday for an hour-long debate hosted by Nexstar's WOOD-TV, beginning at 7 p.m. ET.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Van Gilder, Fox 5

item.title: In battleground states, it's not just about winning — but also losing by less
item.description: In a state where every vote matters, both Democratic and Republican campaigns are not only trying to win in counties where they’re strongest, they’re also trying to lose by less.
item.dc:creator: Ari Shapiro, Mia Venkat, Karen Zamora, and Ashley Brown, MPR News

item.title: Election drop boxes open in San Diego County
item.description: While mail ballots are expected to arrive this week for registered voters, 150 official ballot drop boxes opened Tuesday in San Diego County for the November Presidential General Election.
item.dc:creator: Domenick Candelieri, Fox 5

item.title: Thousands join pro-Palestinian rallies around the globe as Oct. 7 anniversary nears
item.description: Police used tear gas and water cannons to disperse violent demonstrators in Rome as tens of thousands pro-Palestinian protesters took to the streets in European cities and around the globe, calling for a cease-fire as the first anniversary of the Hamas attacks on Israel approached.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News
